title: LESSON.md frontmatter (AIP-11)
description: Validates the YAML frontmatter portion of an AIP-11 LESSON.md file — one transferable lesson distilled from a completed run.

schema.description: Spec identifier. Must be the literal string 'learning/v1'.

slug.description: Machine identifier, also the filename. Lowercase, digits, dashes. Imperative voice recommended.

title.description: One-sentence imperative title — what to do or avoid.

description.description: Plain-text description of when this lesson applies.

tags.description: Retrieval keywords. Keep narrow; three is usually enough.

targets.description: Operator / role / skill globs that scope retrieval. Empty means anyone.

metadata.description: Vendor-specific trigger predicates. Standard fields above MUST NOT be redefined here.

outcome.description: Whether the source run succeeded, failed, or the lesson is conditional across runs.

kind.description: What the evidence reference points to. 'audit' = AIP-7 governance record.

ref.description: Opaque id or path the host can resolve. Never free text.

note.description: One-line factual note about the event — not the lesson.

evidence.description: Provenance — at least one evidence entry MUST resolve to a real run, conversation, work item, audit, or wiki page in the host's indices.

confidence.description: Author's confidence in [0,1]. Default 0.5 at first sighting. Runtimes weigh this against observed counts.

success_count.description: Times the lesson 'worked' when applied. Maintained by the runtime; author-supplied values are initial only.

failure_count.description: Times the lesson's claim was contradicted. Maintained by the runtime; author-supplied values are initial only.

supersedes.description: Slugs of lessons this lesson replaces. Supersession is explicit, never silent. Each cited slug MUST exist on disk.

expires_at.description: Soft TTL (ISO 8601). Past this instant, retrieval treats the lesson as absent by default.

metadata.description: Vendor-specific extensions under namespaced keys (metadata.<vendor>.<field>). Standard fields MUST NOT be redefined.

then.description: A failure-only lesson is permitted with success_count=0 and failure_count>=1, but at least one evidence entry MUST be present (covered by the global minItems constraint).
